reviews: I thought it's a completely different dish so gave them a chance. Not bad. Not the most cultured but the size shocked me. Many curry chicken, for example the one opposite in Queensway shopping centre, eat no full one. This uses the noodle bowl and had lots of tau pok and radish. The chicken, very tender, was a whole leg. Not the best but filling.

This stall tries to ride on the wave of Yan Ji-style seafood soups, but don't be fooled, it's just those run-of-the-mill soups long sold at fish soup stalls. It doesn't really have the requisite ingredients too. There's meatcake, yes, but it's not as good as others. That cloudiness of the soup is only on the surface. They dare sell this from $7.
result.contact_info: 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-22 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

reviews.author: Hui Wen Loon
reviews.text:
Came to try this after a long drinking night.

Extremely friendly auntie who gave lots of nice and fresh ingredients. $13 seafood soup with 3 big prawns, lots of clams and fish. What we liked was the minced meat actually - just look at how much minced meat there was in the soup. Made the soup extra tasty. Perfect for a hangover soup on a rainy day. Very value for money!

reviews.author: Ken Tan
reviews.text: Guess I was the one of those that encounter the grumpy auntie attitude on my first visit.  Fish soup is ok fresh and comes with lots of sliced cabbage.  Taste ok but may avoid just based on experience

reviews.author: Merryheart
reviews.text: The seafood soup was not cheap but ingredients were generous. I paid $16.20 for a big portion with added meatballs which was good to be shared by 2 hungry people. The soup was flavourful with 3 big prawns & thick slices of fish. The meatballs enhanced with flounder fish powder was tasty. It was quite amusing to read the comments of other reviewers about the grumpy auntie.  She did not respond when I placed my order , not sure if she heard me but after she served the customer front of me, she repeated my order correctly.  I guess her mood was not that bad today.

reviews.author: George
reviews.text: Quiet stall but decided to give it a chance, Just wanted seafood soup. Nothing else pictures tell a thousand words. Just give me a proper seafoood soup please:(

reviews.author: Jenny Wong
reviews.text: The seafood soup was flavorful and delicious. The prawns were large, fresh, and succulent, while the fish slices were thick, juicy, and perfectly cooked. The stall auntie expects you to order well, to tell her exactly what you want, so that she can saves some precious time during peak hour. She is also very knowledgeable about her soup. Highly recommended!

result.stall_name: Tiong Bahru Lien Fa Shui Jing Pau (Alexandra Village)
reviews: Savoury turnip sjk 1.1nett: The skin is thicker but that's just as well because the turnip filling is intensely savoury. It's really good, there's ample crunch as well. Chili has a strong tang which goes well w this cos it's intense enough.

Red bean shuijingpau 1.1nett: Thin, very chewy skin without any flavour. The chew isn't unpleasant though, it's not bad at all. It's just a vessel for the red bean paste, which was not too wet and rather earthy.

Teochew Crystal Dumplings ($0.90 for 1): My dining companion actually commented that they used to had more mushrooms and dried shrimps in their filling. Similar to @ieatishootipost's review though, their skin is indeed not sticky or gooey, but thick and chewy.
The thin gelatinous skin has a distinctive kind of sticky chewiness that I adore. To prevent it from easily tearing, oil is applied to the dumplings immediately after they are steamed.
Dumplings!!: It’s chewy and so good ❤️ wanted to try the red bean dumpling but it was only available in the afternoon 😌
result.contact_info: +65 62745561; 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-10 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

google.google_name: Tiong Bahru Lien Fa Shui Jing Pau

reviews.author: Ken Tan
reviews.text:
First time trying as previous visits were sold out.  Tried all 3 flavours - turnip, red bean and yam.  Perfect the sweet flavours.  Skin is chewy quite interesting though some parts may be a bit tough/thick.
Good taste in general for a snack though the size is quite small @ $1.2-1.3 each

reviews.author: Alan Tang
reviews.text:
I must say the quality of the Shui Jing Pau had dropped.
The chopped turnips were “rough”, it used to be quite small chips.
Flavor had totally changed - perhaps lacking of meat, mushrooms and dried shrimps.
My box of 10 pieces cost $12; which is rather expensive.
Rather disappointed with their standard - they used to be famous with customers queueing to buy.

reviews.author: Veronica Ong
reviews.text: Absolutely outstanding. The skin was impossibly thin yet perfectly structured, and the filling was unmistakably hand cut with bold, incredible flavour. The chilli on the side was spot on, just the right kick to elevate every bite. You can feel the craftsmanship and heart behind this. Pure artisanal excellence. Support this gem before it disappears.

reviews.author: Stanley Pang
reviews.text: Ordered the jicama fillings. Skin is nice and chewy, while fillings has a unique lard aroma.

reviews.author: Flo G
reviews.text:
A nostalgic childhood taste that brings lots of good memories. This diminishing trade which seems that no where else has.
So for all the poor reviews, we cut them some slack.

Only manage to get the last of the savoury ones close to 1pm, sold out after.

Skin is thin and QQ, Taste is still as good as I remember. So good the chill..

Love the sweet versions, do call to reserve before heading down to save disappointment.

Don’t expect service or warm hospitality, won’t be disappointed.

$1.10 each min. 4 pcs at Feb24

Definitely coming back to satisfy my sweet cravings for their redbean & yam paste.

result.stall_name: Yong Kee Famous Fish Ball Noodle (Alexandra Village)
reviews: Fishball soup 4nett
One Trick Pony (referring to fishball noodles)
result.contact_info: 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-26 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

reviews.author: Ty L
reviews.text: Stopped by Ding Sheng, unit #01-78. Great service by this 'uncle'. Ordered Mui Fan, $5.50. Average, nothing exceptional. Tried Aunty Jo's dessert, ice jelly with sea coconut, $2.80. This place gets a little crowded from 7pm onwards. Also try Hane Chiang Thai food , #01-21. Tom Yum soup is ok.

reviews.author: Eddie Kok-Leong Teoh
reviews.text:
Very nice prawn noodle. Good size of prawn. I tried the 3 in 1. Prawn, Pork Rib and Pig Tail.
The soup is fantastic. The chili powder lift the taste to next level.
The uncle in front of me order 3 packs. Took out some time. Worth the queue though. 👍

Ps: we finished the food before the review.

Desert : this cendol is authentic. The cendol seem hand made by the auntie who sells the desert.

Satisfying brunch 😋 today !

reviews.author: Mr Gao
reviews.text:
Nice bowl of claypot laksa. Flavourful broth. Can add chilli myself to my liking.
Portion is just nice 👍 still have stomach space for other nice foods/drinks around the hawker centre.

reviews.author: Donald Chen
reviews.text:
Ordered the following
1. Pan fried dumplings
2. Szechuan spicy wanton
3. Spicy and sour soup

I like the spicy wanton the best, followed by pan fried dumplings.

reviews.author: Wong Kuang Hua
reviews.text:
Awesome Comfort food
Soon Seng Coffee - One of the Best Kopi Kao.
JJ Fishball Noodles:
Mee Tai Mak soup and Dried Mee Pok are excellent.

Thunder Tea Rice

reviews: Mutton soup. Tbh for a moment I barely realised it's mutton, that's how much their herbal soup is absorbing.
Stewed beef. There's something about their beef stew that gains my appreciation the more I have it.
Mixed Beef soup 8nett. Smaller portion available. Two huge soft beef balls, and everything else was tender.
Small Beef Stew 6nett. Very generous portion, beef was tender for this lean cut and the tendons were soft.
result.contact_info: +65 97888331; 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-16 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

reviews.author: Super Fragrance
reviews.text:
Ordered $15 beef and tendon stew plus oyster vege for $5.

Honestly the oyster vege is 5 out of 5 stars i love it. The beef and tendon was quite on point. Some of the beef just melt in the mouth. They are pretty good with or without the chili.

However just pretty good lack the wow and potion is a bit little for $15

reviews.author: Thaddeus Zheng
reviews.text:
Soft and tender beef. Slight salty for my taste, this pairing with rice. Chef included some veg's to make me feel the meal is less guilty.

Comfort food is what I can say.

reviews.author: TF
reviews.text: Love the dry egg noodles with tender stew beef brisket and sauce all over it. Seeing the black le creuset pot containing the beef brisket is already a turn on. Ate here a few years ago before leaving overseas and came back to the same taste. Added two beef balls on my own. Love the chili with strong hints of lime yet remaining spicy. Pretty unique. Small bowl of accompanying soup came piping hot with flavorful beef aroma. My number one recommendation if you are into beef brisket noodles.

reviews.author: Lydia Wang
reviews.text:
Herbal mix beef soup. This is really good. I had the $6 portion. It was extremely tasty and affordable and Value for money.

Previously I was going to blanco beef noodle but this is better. The soup is really thick and authentic. Oily too because of the beef.

But they are very generous with the ingredients for a $6 portion. If u are a big eater, get the $7 portion.

Will come back again if I'm nearby.

reviews.author: Han Sheng Ler
reviews.text:
It's hawker!
Best beef stew. Soft and have layered texture. The tendon melts in your mouth.

The uncle looks fierce. But he's friendly.
Even asked if you want soup.
Get the soup. Complete the set!

result.stall_name: Dover Road Kai Kee Wanton Noodle (Alexandra Village)
reviews: Wanton Mee ($3.50). Tasted q old school. The noodles weren’t really springy, but I liked the simple sauce.
Old School Wanton Mee. Dover Road Kai Kee wanton mee first opened over 30 years ago at the now demolished Dover Road market.
Wanton Noodle. Kai Kee Wanton Noodle uses a traditional Chinese Wine sauce instead of the more common versions.
Good Noodles, Bad Char Siew. Unfortunately this famous stall doesn't tick all the boxes.
result.contact_info: 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-09 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

google.google_name: 杜佛路佳纪云吞面 Dover Road Kai Kee Wanton Noodles

reviews.author: CH Pow
reviews.text: Generous portion. Noodles is very nice. Just nicely cooked and without any alkaline smell, egg noodles goodness. The sauce is balanced and savoury ; not overly sweet. Wanton is good too. Whilst the char siew is not the HK style char siew, it’s far better than the typical “red colored” piece of paper thin lean pork. Soup is very bland though.

reviews.author: edtayjb
reviews.text: This wantan mee stall in Alexandra Village Food Centre has been here for the longest time - serving up very good, old-school wantan mee without the "frills" found at modern day wantan mee eateries. Everything here is "simple and basic" but nostalgically good and yummy. Service at this stall is nice enough and the same can be said about the dining ambience here at Alexandra Village Food Centre.

reviews.author: Ars 3276
reviews.text:
Run be very old couple serving old school wtm.
Generous portion given.

Chilli was not too bad but the rest wasn't to my taste buds.
Soup was cold but overall Glad to have tried it.

reviews.author: James Lee
reviews.text:
I will tell you what it is.
This is an acquired taste.
The first time I ate it, I felt it was super oily, fillings little, char siew, wanton all cmi. Noodle was not your usual wanton egg noodle you see in roasted meat stalls.

I decided to try again because it was supposed to be recommended so why am I so disappointed?
Surprisingly, this time, I ate slower, tasting the noodle’s texture and taste in my mouth. Try to taste the minute details. The sesame oil especially, iconic of a Cantonese dish. Eat each part on its own and then together slowly.
The feeling was different. The oily feel and choice of noodles, etc all made sense.
Since then, I was in love with them and is my go-to wanton mee stall.

Side note: their chopsticks and spoons have a bad smell because they cover it with a wet towel. I always wash them before using.

Other than that, I felt I really acquired the taste of it.

reviews.author: L Chan
reviews.text: Tried the dry wanton noodles for $4. Portions were generous, though the flavour leaned mild with a slight alkaline note. Some call it old-school, but it may not suit everyone’s taste.

reviews: Oyster Peanut Vadai. Chef introduced this to me before he opened his second stall.
Red Sea Prawn Hokkien Mee. Chef goo has a rather extensive menu, from noodles, to pao fan.
Seafood Pao Fan. Chef goo has a rather extensive menu, from noodles, to pao fan, to fried rice.
Overpriced and too heavy taste. Price of the braised pork belly noodle is around $12.
result.contact_info: +65 88066742; 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-13 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

reviews.author: sl wong
reviews.text: Ordered caipoh steam fish which took close to 20 min to be done, hence might be good to order something else if short of time. Fish was so so. Abit salty for me but decent. Note that it's only half a fish. Mine has rosy pink cheks

reviews.author: Jason Soh
reviews.text: We ordered the sambal fish, fried hokkien mee and fried kway tiao. Overall, all 3 dishes are pretty average, none of the 3 tastes exceptionally well or bad. But given the amount of good quality stalls in the alexandra food centre, i can understand why most people will rate it lower than average.

reviews.author: 葉
reviews.text: Don’t be confused by the wide array of items on their menu, the star dish remains to be the fried Hokkien mee. The umami-filled broth is infused with the flavours of the sea and smoke, and fresh sweet prawns, pork slices, and clams cooked nicely with a healthy serving of noodles.

reviews.author: Raymond Toh
reviews.text: Had a very bad experience with the owner of this hawker. I had brought my family and friends for a gathering at this hawker centre for well known for good foods. We had ordered varieties of foods to share so as to get a taste for many. However, we were taken aback of a plate of fried noodles from Chef Goo that was tasted of over burnt rather than wokhey. Feedback to the owner but he denied and rudely said that because we ate it already so no replacement. I asked him to have a taste of the over burnt noodle but he totally ignored. Is this the way to treat your customers? We will never patronise again for such arrogance hawker.

reviews.author: Gopi Bala
reviews.text:
There have been mixed reviews for the Hokkien mee sold here.

Today I decided to tar pow 2 bowls of the noodles, which were consumed some 3.5 hrs after it was cooked.

I found it very good and they were generous with the prawns, lala and pork belly. The chilli provided was also good.

If the stock used could be more flavourful, I would rate it 5 stars.

The Hokkien mee was great value for money for me.

Will definitely come back to try the other dishes.

reviews: Always can’t resist AJ Delights’ stall smell whenever I pass by. Muffins are baked to perfection, fluffy and soft!
Double Chocolate Muffin. I finally got here after perhaps 10 attempts. This muffin was so ordinary.
Vanilla Streusel Muffin 2.3nett. A very good muffin. The texture was spot on.
Group Buy Streusel Lover (S$17.45) 6 assorted muffins topped with streusel.
result.contact_info: +65 97525973; 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-82/83 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

reviews.author: Harlee Q
reviews.text:
It’s been donkey years since I last had their muffins at a hawker centre in Queenstown. It was one of the better ones I had then and they still are today. I chanced upon their pop up stall at either Jem or Westgate in the last week of Feb. I have to say their muffins tasted better in the sense it’s not loaded with sugar, it’s moist and there’s more variety than I last had a decade ago. Take apple crumble muffin for example, it literally has sliced apples in the muffin,  shredded gula coconut in the ondeh x 2 ones too. The best thing is their muffins are still soft after being left at room temperature for 2 days.

Last but not least, the people behind the stall - a Chinese lady and I believe a Malay gentleman at the pop up stall rock! Both are engaging and are damn good in sales (not the pushy way). The kind of employees all bosses would want to bring onboard. I ended up buying 3 boxes of muffins that week. Two boxes between my kid and I and the other to gift.

reviews.author: Mohamed Haniffa
reviews.text:
Was nearby, so headed down to have a look.
Person at stall greeted me. Went in for a closer look. They were only double choco and chocolate Hazelnut on the shelf. Just wanted to grab one muffin but ended buying the assorted muffins. 6 for $15.90. Good deal. No two boxes have the similar flavours. Tried the ondeh ondeh one for starters. The fillings are little actually. Taste was nice. Not bad. Would recommend to heat up lightly before eating.

reviews.author: Amelia Crowe
reviews.text:
Amazing muffins, I had been buying for years!
Great for breakfast when heated up, or an afternoon snack.
Fav flavours such as:
- Walnut Banana Vanilla
- Walnut Banana Double Chocolate
- Cookies N Creme
- Chocolate Hazelnut
- Green Tea Original
- Banana Streusel
- Walnut Streusel Double Chocolate
- Mocha Walnut Streusel
- Biscoff (Biscoff Pillow)
In the picture is: Walnut Streusel Double Chocolate muffin
The owner and employees were friendly and accommodating.
Fluffy and moist textures, along with not too sweet, are always addicting to eat.

reviews.author: Touristmouse
reviews.text: I recall seeing their mall pop-up and decided to give them a try after walking past their hawker stall. I got the Double Joy for $3. It is a tad pricey for a muffin, but still feels reasonable for the quality. The muffin is fluffy and moist. While it is slightly sweet and sinful, it is definitely worth a treat on cheat days. Accepts Shopback Pay.

reviews.author: Afni Raeimi
reviews.text: They were able to accommodate to my 40 muffins walk in order. Owner and staff were very friendly and helpful, ensuring I have the correct nut-free order. They included a handful of other flavours for me to enjoy, complimentary. Definitely coming back, especially for bulk orders. Everybody loves a delicious muffin! ❤️

result.stall_name: Wei Zhong Qi Prawn Noodle (Alexandra Village)
reviews: Prawn noodles. Old style prawn mee. Average in taste. Try if you must.
result.contact_info: 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-87 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

reviews.author: Ambrose Cheng
reviews.text:
Flavorful and full of aroma! Nice soup and tender succulent pork! Worth coming back just for the pork haha 😁
If you come here expecting big prawn noodles style food, you might be disappointed. For a start, they don’t have thick bee hoon! But as a unique dish, this is simply delicious! The pork is tender and their kway teow is uniquely thick, making the blend of texture and flavors a delightful adventure for the tongue.
Though I ate prawn noodles, given how tender and tasty their pork is, I’d recommend giving their pork ribs noodles a try! That’s definitely on my list of things to try the next time I’m here :)

reviews.author: Philip Ng
reviews.text: My favourite prawn noodle at Alexandra Village. In fact I think it's probably the best in Singapore. The soup is really to die for. I usually order the dry version and add lots of fried shallots mixed with fried pork lard into the both the soup and noodles 😋 So yummy

reviews.author: Timothy TB Chua
reviews.text: Been eating here for 30 years. The old man has long since passed away and the stall has been handed over to his helper. My favorite prawn noodles in Singapore. The soup is unbelievable. So good. Auntie(the old helper) is getting on lately, some prawns not cleaned properly. Poor lady can’t see so well anymore 😢

reviews.author: Hulk
reviews.text: Tried the dry version of the pork rib prawn noodles. The chilli lacks fragrance and its colour… but the portion of the noodles was generous. Reckon the soup version is a more popular choice.

reviews.author: Matt S
reviews.text:
Ordered for the first time. Was hungry so I went for the $5 option.

The quantity of the noodles was great and the soup was flavorful. Unfortunately, the noodles I received was extremely overcooked, to the point where the texture was soggy. As hungry as I was, the overcooked noodles killed my appetite and I couldn't even finish half of it.

I went after lunchtime and there were no other customers, so there were no distractions whatsoever to explain the lack of care in preparing the noodles.

2 stars given on account of the quantity of noodles and the flavorful shop.

reviews: Henghwa Bee Hoon. The only place which I would get my putien bee Hoon / lor mee / mee swa from.
Pig Trotter Beehoon. I knew this wasn't the local version, but I still ordered half-hoping it is.
Fried Eggplant With Floss. Back here again and we ordered a few new dishes to try.
Incredulously Bland. If you expect the Heng Hua Fried Beehoon to be more flavourful than it looks.
result.contact_info: 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-83 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

reviews.author: edtayjb
reviews.text: I love this Putien cuisine stall in Alexandra Village Food Centre. Although it serves up a very wide selection of Putien dishes, I almost always order the same thing here and that is its "Putien Crispy Oysters" because it is excellently prepared and both the taste and texture of it is simply sublime and if I might add, value for money. Service here at this stall is nice enough and so is the dining ambience at Alexandra Village Food Centre.

reviews.author: Chee Wan Lim
reviews.text:
Ordered the Putien Shimp Rolls, Putien Sweet Sour Lychee Pork, Deep Fried Tenggiri Fish, Putien Crispy Oyster and Xing Hua Bee Hoon.
The Shrimp Rolls' batter is too thick with little fillings
The Pork does not have lychee and merely tasted like stirred in ketchup sauce without any serious stirred fried.
Tenggiri Fish can be done better with stronger fire to give it a crispy exterior.
Oyster egg is done sufficiently well and Bee Hoon is average.

reviews.author: Wang Huiyuan
reviews.text: Happened to walk pass this sir in Alexandra Village Hawker Centre. Had putian food before but aeons ago.  Try and are if they still taste same?  Ordered noodle and beehoon fried.  The noodle soup was OK though less flavourful. They include clams,  prawn,  small slices of taupok and cabbages. Bee hoon though was abit dry,  with similar ingredients. Can try again if in the vicinity. Finished the food with a bowl of Nonya Chendol,  which was good,  better than the steal in shampoos Whapoa Hawker Centre.

reviews.author: Curious Panda
reviews.text:
quite generous amount of bee hoon and ingredients for the price. There is prawns, clams, tau kee, white cabbage, seaweed and peanut.

slightly charred wok hei, and not too oily Xing Hua Bee Hoon. already taste good on its own, but recommend half original and half with the chilli so you get best of both worlds.

reviews.author: Ann G
reviews.text: Ordered the lor mee. Presentation looked good. But the soup lacks the umami seafood taste. Only 3 small prawns, few clams, some taupok, veg and peanuts. Noodle portion was fine and taste not too bad. $5 for small bowl. Maybe my expectation was raised too highly by Putien restaurant.

result.stall_name: Leon Kee Claypot Pork Rib Soup (Alexandra Village)
reviews: Pork Rib Soup. This place serves Hokkien style BKT soup that uses soy sauce and herbs.
Pigtail. I didn't think this was particularly impressive, it didn't taste like too much honestly.
Spare ribs bkt. Generous amount of meat, 4 big pieces and 2 small pieces. It's reasonably tender.
Sesame oil chicken. It's pretty well executed, reasonably tender and quite a unique sauce.
result.contact_info: 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-18 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

google.google_name: Leon Kee Claypot Pork Rib Soup

reviews.author: Crumpled Nomad
reviews.text:
Leon Kee Claypot Pork Rib Soup is one of those rare food stalls that truly feels timeless. I first ate here more than 20 years ago, back when it was still the old uncle personally cooking behind the stove. Returning now brings back many memories, and it’s heartening to see that the legacy has clearly been carried on. The lady running the stall today appears to be his daughter, who I remember used to help serve customers back then — a beautiful continuation of a family craft.

The claypot pork rib soup tastes just as I remember it: comforting, hearty, and full of depth. The broth is rich yet clean, infused with peppery warmth and slow-cooked goodness that seeps right into the meat. It’s the kind of soup that feels nourishing and grounding, especially on a long day.

Seeing Leon Kee recognised in the Michelin Guide 2025 feels well deserved. This isn’t trendy food — it’s honest, traditional cooking done right, and consistently so over decades. A bowl of history, care, and comfort in every serving.

reviews.author: Kenny Sng
reviews.text: Nice tasting pork ribs soup. Soup is hearty and you are given 5 pieces of ribs. Will be great to get some small pieces of mushroom or tau kee to make a complete meal. $8 which people will benchmark against other meals in the food center. Tricky to price the meal but I appreciate the quality of the food.

reviews.author: Alan Goh
reviews.text:
Overall lunch experience is below average.

The Hawker Centre has reopened on 1-July 2025.

Ordered the Kway Chup ($5) served in styrofoam plate and a bowl of Kway.

Stall boss uncle seems to be chanting as he walked around the stall front … and the stall lady boss aunty isn’t a tad bit friendly.

All the utensils and the chilli sauce have to be arranged and served by the stall uncle boss

1st food bite … cannot already.
And get a low score of 1-star.

Nothing positive to add on and won’t patronize this stall again.

Will allow this hawker to vanish by natural selection.

reviews.author: Jacqueline Lee
reviews.text: Leon Kee serves authentic hokkien style bak kut teh with delish side dishes. The fruit stall beside is also famous for their fruits/veg juices especially avocado shake! 🥑 Recommend all to try 😃

reviews.author: Mike Ang
reviews.text:
Not sure why but got Michelin star , so try loh. Not sure how to rate this Bakut teh.... since young till now almost like 40yrs eating Bakut teh, from
Malaysia kalang to Singapore.... only taste of Bakut teh which I cannot tell .... not near to the tang gue taste and not near to any of the Bakut teh but anyway still a lot of meat given. Thks

reviews: Avocado Milkshake. The avocado milkshake is thick and creamy in texture with a distinct taste of avocado.
King Size. I've started to appreciate avocado drinks more and more. This giant cup is only $6.
Avocado Smoothie 🥑. Famous for their avocado drinks, there are multiple stalls within Alexandra.
result.contact_info: 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-17 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

reviews.author: Yan C.
reviews.text:
Decent avocado drink, very fragrant (on top of gula melaka, it has a hint of pandan?)
However, the lady boss was dishonest, ordered a large one and immediately realised it is a super huge cup. So I told her to change to medium one instead (within 1 minute), the lady boss said started making already, cant change. But I saw clearly the uncle working behind was making a watermelon drink (red color, not green)!

reviews.author: Mei Mei Tay
reviews.text:
Medium, no ice no milk, asked for less sugar but the auntie say cannot because this is pre-made. It’s very watery too with very little avocado taste.

Tastes sweet enough to be disgusting, a bit sour (probably cause the gula melaka is old) and the price $7 is way too expensive for this quality.

reviews.author: Joyce Li
reviews.text: I prefer this stall than the other one. I always order less sweet and no sugar option. Equally yummy.

reviews.author: JR FF
reviews.text:
Nice avocado shake here.

Was too sweet for me - but for gula melaka fans, thats the shake for you.

reviews.author: Abraham
reviews.text: Pretty decent and refreshing. Good avocado mix, and had many options of avocado mixes. Store auntie was quite friendly and polite. Place accepts CDC vouchers as well. Best part was that it’s opened around 7ish as well.

reviews: Beef Hor Fun@$5.50. Returned for seconds. Got consistency, really good wok hei.
Mixed Hor Fun @$5.50. Strong Wok Hei. Cheap and affordable also. One of the must have at Alexandra.
Beef horfun 5.5nett. Masterful. Beef was tender with an appropriate level of soft chew.
Good Hor Fun!. Looks like a sloppy mess, but I can guarantee that you will be wiping the gravy clean.
result.contact_info: 120 Bukit Merah Lane 1 #01-78 Alexandra Village Food Centre Singapore 150120

reviews.author: Yi Ling
reviews.text:
This isn't my first time coming here to eat after 7pm, and it is a crowd favorite, given that there's always a long queue that moves quickly here.

​They only serve 2 items: Hor Fun (a flat rice noodle dish) and Mui Fan (white rice with starch slurry) The ingredients are either seafood, fish, or beef. The pricing point here averages around $5.50, which is rarely affordable and really good in terms of portion!

​The iconic part about the hor fun is that it has really strong and aromatic wok hei, which is achieved by heating the wok thoroughly such that the smokiness seeps into the wide rice noodles to give it a charred, aromatic flavor. You can tell that the smokiness of this hawker center all comes from this stall when you walk here, as there's a cloud of smoke coming from it.

Nonetheless, the hor fun here were really good, and it goes with the thick starch slurry to give it a simple, homey, yet at the same time fulfilling and satisfying dish. Portion sizes & ingredients are quite generous and very filling too!

reviews.author: Marcus Nah
reviews.text:
Nice beef hor fan.. Kway Teow wok hei good.. Beef portion also rather quite generous for the price of $5.50.

Expect some queues at 5.40pm..  waiting time around 10mins.

reviews.author: Yizhou Wu
reviews.text:
PSA: they moved inside and now the original location is another hor fun stall, so pls don't be mislead

Review:
The roar of the wok tells you everything you want to know about the horfun. They ladel a super hot egg sauce over the pre fried horfun, so service is fast. The horfun has a lot of wok hey which is brought out by the heat of the sauce. Beef was tender, and generous

reviews.author: S Wun
reviews.text: Nov 2025: what a chance find and we will come again! Definitely one of the best hor fun I have tried and I have tried many! There are long lines so get into the queue quickly. They move quickly though as the cooking process is broken down into parts for efficiency (with no lack in taste!) talk about specialisation! Yummy and has wok hei!

reviews.author: Ivan Tan
reviews.text:
One of the best beef hor fun I’ve even eaten!
Ordered the beef hor fun($5.50) and was blown away by the amount of beef given, thick slices and well marinated… the hor fun is well cooked with strong taste of wok hei. Very well cooked!
